entities.name: WALKLEY-BLACK METHOD
entities.description: A modified chemical method used for the simultaneous determination of organic carbon and potentially mineralizable nitrogen in soils

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon content of organic matter in soil, determined via oxidation

entities.name: POTENTIALLY MINERALIZABLE NITROGEN
entities.description: The nitrogen in soil that can be converted to ammonium through oxidative action

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop associated with the tropical soils analyzed in the study

entities.name: TROPICAL
entities.description: The climatic region characterizing the soils used in the study

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium analyzed for organic carbon and nitrogen content

entities.name: ACID DICHROMATE REAGENT
entities.description: A reagent used to oxidize organic matter in soil by heat of dilution

entities.name: FERROUS SULFATE SOLUTION
entities.description: A standard solution used for titration to avoid ammonium contamination

entities.name: AMMONIUM
entities.description: The NH4+ released during the oxidative action of acid dichromate

entities.name: NAOH SOLUTION
entities.description: A 50% solution used for distilling ammonium from the soil aliquot

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total nitrogen content in the soil, showing high correlation with potentially mineralizable nitrogen

entities.name: WATERLOGGED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions under which ammonium production is compared
